title: Verilator recognizes wire delay as a function error
body:
Hello!

I encountered a problem during CVA6 core simulation with Verilator (5.041 devel rev v5.040-217-g98786e609). The error is:
```
%Error: /home/username/cva6/corev_apu/tb/common/SimDTM.sv:41:15: Delays are not legal in functions. Suggest use a task (IEEE 1800-2023 13.4.4)
                                                                                     : ... note: In instance 'ariane_testharness'
   41 |   wire [31:0] #0.1 __debug_resp_bits_data = debug_resp_bits_data;
      |               ^
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.041 for more assistance.
```

I tried simulations with different Verilator releases (version 5.008 as required by CVA6, and the latest release 5.040), but this error only appears in version 5.041. Additionally, the error description "Delays are not legal in functions" appears to be misleading.

After modifying the SystemVerilog file SimDTM.sv as follows:
```
wire [31:0] __debug_resp_bits_data;
assign #0.1 __debug_resp_bits_data = debug_resp_bits_data;
```
the problem disappears. However, I would prefer not to modify the CVA6 source files directly.

Could you please take a look at the error?
